learnings.subject: computer-science
learnings.title: Process vs Thread
learnings.summary: A process is an independent program with its own memory; threads are lightweight units that share their process's memory.
learnings.content:
## Key differences

| | Process | Thread |
|---|---|---|
| Memory | Own address space | Shared within the process |
| Creation cost | High | Low |
| Crash impact | Isolated | Can take down the whole process |

## Why it matters
Threads make concurrency cheap because they share memory, but that sharing is exactly why you need synchronization (locks, mutexes) to avoid race conditions.

learnings.subject: programming
learnings.title: Big-O Notation
learnings.summary: Big-O describes how an algorithm's running time grows as the input grows, ignoring constants.
learnings.content:
## Common classes (fast to slow)
- O(1) — constant
- O(log n) — logarithmic
- O(n) — linear
- O(n log n) — linearithmic
- O(n^2) — quadratic
